speaker: A
lines: {A} charges in again. {B} sighs.
{A} goes one round too many. {B} looks away.
{A} steps forward with a grin. {B} quietly asks them to reconsider.
{A} cracks their knuckles eagerly. {B} winces at the implication.
{A} mutters that they 'want more'. {B} shakes their head slowly.
{A} leans toward the fight again. {B} whispers a plea for restraint.
reply: That wasn't necessary, {A}. Not this time either.
You don't have to chase every fight, {A}.
Please slow down, {A}. This isn't the way.
I know you feel it, {A}, but violence isn't purpose.

speaker: A
lines: {A} is already recounting the shares, out loud. {B} frowns.
{A} mentions a "little extra" nobody had brought up. {B} doesn't like it.
{A} wonders if the client 'forgot' to mention a bonus. {B} doubts it.
{A} suggests checking pockets 'just in case'. {B} frowns deeply.
{A} asks if they can renegotiate. {B} sighs.
{A} eyes the loot pile. {B} crosses their arms.
reply: That's not what we agreed to, {A}.
We're not squeezing people for more, {A}.
We agreed on fairness, {A}. Stick to it.
No shortcuts, {A}. Not with the truth.

speaker: A
lines: {A} already has fists clenched. {B} takes a step back.
{A} raises their voice. {B} is already scanning for the exit.
{A} stomps forward. {B} nearly jumps.
{A} growls under their breath. {B} edges away.
{A} slams a door open. {B} flinches hard.
{A} threatens action. {B} begs them not to.
reply: Calm down, {A}, we're all going to pay for this.
I really don't want to be here if you explode, {A}.
I'm begging you, {A}. Don't start something.

speaker: A
lines: {A} wants to end it fast and hard. {B} raises a hand: not like this.
{A} ignores the usual restraint. {B} doesn't like the method.
{A} charges ahead. {B} calls out a reminder of honor.
{A} raises their blade. {B} insists on proper form.
{A} pushes forward aggressively. {B} demands discipline.
{A} ignores caution. {B} warns them of dishonor.
reply: There's a right way to do things, {A}.
We don't hit an opponent who's down, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} checks the emergency exit three times. {B} shrugs, amused.
{A} jumps at the slightest noise. {B} clearly finds it funny.
{A} checks behind them again. {B} strolls confidently.
{A} whispers a fear. {B} laughs it off.
{A} hesitates at a doorway. {B} walks through untouched.
{A} expects disaster. {B} expects luck.
reply: Relax, {A}, it always works out for me.
Don't worry, {A}, I've never had a problem with that.
You worry too much, {A}. I've got this.
Things fall my way, {A}. They always do.

speaker: A
lines: {A} is already recalculating their cut after {B} closed the deal.
{A} whispers that they could have gotten more out of {B}, once the deal was signed.
{A} asks if {B} could push harder next time. {B} smirks knowingly.
{A} wonders if the client had more to give. {B} shrugs.
{A} suggests a 'bonus clause'. {B} laughs softly.
{A} hints they could have doubled the price. {B} rolls their eyes.
reply: We got a good price, {A}. Be happy with that.
That was already a good deal, {A}.
Trust me, {A}, I pushed as far as we could.
More pressure would've broken the deal, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} calls out {B} on a slightly embellished detail of their story.
{A} points out, a bit too loudly, that it's not exactly what happened.
{A} corrects another detail mid‑sentence. {B} groans softly.
{A} points out a contradiction. {B} waves it off.
{A} insists on truth. {B} insists on presentation.
{A} highlights a factual error. {B} calls it 'creative framing'.
reply: Let's just say I smoothed out the edges, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} already wants to answer with force. {B} puts a firm hand on their shoulder.
{A} clenches their fists before the discussion is even over. {B} shakes their head.
{A} raises their voice. {B} keeps theirs steady.
{A} pushes forward. {B} holds them back.
{A} fumes loudly. {B} stays composed.
{A} threatens action. {B} cites the code.
reply: Not like that, {A}. We have rules.
Breathe, {A}. That's not how we do things.
The code is stronger than anger, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} refuses to deliver the finishing blow. {B} insists: honor demands it.
{A} and {B} disagree on what "doing things right" really means.
{A} steps back. {B} urges them forward.
{A} hesitates. {B} insists on ritual.
{A} questions the code. {B} defends it.
{A} refuses the strike. {B} calls it dishonor.
reply: Honor sometimes demands finishing what you started, {A}.
This isn't cruelty, {A}, it's the code.

speaker: A
lines: {A} takes a step back. {B} takes a hit without even flinching.
{A} watches {B} get back up like nothing happened, in disbelief.
{A} panics at a scratch. {B} shrugs.
{A} expects {B} to fall. {B} stays upright.
{A} worries loudly. {B} barely reacts.
{A} hides behind {B}. {B} stands firm.
reply: Don't worry about me, {A}. Worry about them.

speaker: A
lines: {A} already follows {B}'s gaze toward something shiny in the corner.
{A} asks {B} to "keep an eye" on anything that might be worth something.
{A} points excitedly at a glimmer. {B} doesn't break focus.
{A} suggests checking a drawer. {B} shakes their head.
{A} hints at hidden treasure. {B} keeps eyes on the mission.
{A} asks if they can 'quickly grab' something. {B} refuses.
reply: I saw it, {A}. We'll see if we have time.
We're not here to collect souvenirs, {A}.
I'll tell you when it's safe, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} just made everything blow up. {B} steps in to salvage the situation.
{A} raises their voice one more time. {B} steps in with a disarming smile.
{A} escalates. {B} de-escalates instantly.
{A} pushes tension higher. {B} diffuses it smoothly.
{A} makes things worse. {B} patches it with words.
{A} scares the crowd. {B} reassures them effortlessly.
reply: I'll fix what you just broke, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} is already leaning toward the fallen opponent's belongings. {B} steps in the way.
{A} wants to search the defeated opponent's pockets. {B} finds it disgraceful.
{A} crouches to inspect loot. {B} pulls them back.
{A} suggests 'just a quick look'. {B} refuses.
{A} tries again. {B} stands firm.
{A} argues it's 'just practical'. {B} calls it dishonor.
reply: We don't loot a defeated opponent, {A}.
That's not how we do things, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} is already looking for a way out. {B} stands firm, unmoving.
{A} suggests retreating. {B} refuses to turn their back this soon.
{A} panics at movement. {B} stays steady.
{A} urges withdrawal. {B} refuses with dignity.
{A} hides behind debris. {B} stands tall.
{A} whispers fear. {B} whispers courage.
reply: There's a right way to withdraw, {A}, and this isn't it.

speaker: A
lines: {A} throws themselves into the fray again. {B} comes out unscathed, as always.
{A} has lost count of the risks taken. {B} shrugs, amused by their own luck.
{A} dives into danger. {B} strolls out untouched.
{A} pushes too far. {B} laughs at their luck.
{A} nearly gets hit. {B} avoids it casually.
{A} takes risks. {B} avoids consequences.
reply: Another time that could have gone badly, {A}. Another time it didn't.
Don't worry, {A}, I've got good karma right now.
I always land on my feet, {A}.

speaker: A
lines: {A} already wants to get back into it. {B} just takes the hits and waits.
{A} is fuming, ready to explode. {B} stays stoic, like nothing happened.
{A} raises their voice. {B} doesn't flinch.
{A} steps forward. {B} stays calm.
{A} vents frustration. {B} absorbs it.
{A} pushes conflict. {B} remains unshaken.
reply: Why are you getting worked up, {A}? It barely does anything to me.
Save your energy, {A}. I'll take the hits, you save yourself.

speaker: A
lines: {A} quietly steps back while {B} speaks up to defuse the situation.
{A} lets {B} negotiate, relieved not to have to lift a finger.
{A} stays silent. {B} takes control.
{A} trembles slightly. {B} reassures everyone.
{A} whispers fear. {B} speaks confidence.
{A} steps back again. {B} steps forward.
reply: Don't worry, {A}, I've got this. We don't need it to come to that.
Let me talk, {A}. Nobody needs to fight.
